checks.finding: Every inline technology/architecture decision in design.md carries an explicit rationale, typically citing an INV-xxx investigation finding, an ADR, decision-document section, or a named adversarial-review finding. Examples: 'No new plugin' is justified by 'Design Decisions, Epic A2 precedent, INV-013' (Components); '.yaml, not .json' is justified in Design Decisions ('git-diff readability for a human reviewing a PR... the same reasoning Epic A1 gives for project-context.yaml'); 'No .js wrapper for any of the four new scripts' is justified by the digest-primitive-vs-comparator distinction (Global Constraints); 'registry_digest binds via --whole' has a full soundness argument (Design Decisions, citing INV-019); Python stdlib-only is justified by INV-014 (no jsonschema anywhere in the repo). No unjustified 'we will use X' statement was found.

design.md's '## Open Questions' section (line 1472) points to requirements.md's single entry, OQ-002 (requirements.md line 1389): owner is named as 'an Epic A5 CI-wiring decision' (a role/epic, not TBD); it explicitly states it 'does not block this feature's own schema/validator design' (equivalent to Blocks Implementation: no); the resolution path is stated in substance ('CI-gated drift check vs. on-demand', to be decided when Epic A5 implements the Resolver) though not under a literal 'Resolution Path:' label. Per this check's own calibration ('Open questions that do not block implementation and have a stated owner are acceptable Minor findings if incomplete'), the missing literal field labels are non-blocking. OQ-001 was retired into Non-goals (investigation.md, requirements.md) and is not carried as an open question. No Major finding warranted.

investigation.md is present (not absent), so the absent-investigation Major-finding trigger does not apply. requirements.md's '## Assumptions' section (line 1349) lists 4 entries, each grounded in a cited INV-xxx or a specific sibling-epic spec section: (1) Epic A1 canonicalizer shape cites INV-008 directly; (2) Epic A2 Registry schema/Evidence shape cites 'specs/epic-190-a2-capability-registry/design.md's already-Passed shape'; (3) Epic A3's --facet-manifest read contract cites INV-006 directly; (4) a future Registry/ownership weakening detector is explicitly flagged as an accepted, explicitly-stated risk (cross-referenced to the 'Ambiguity-resolution risk' in requirements.md Risks and 'Policy-Weakening short-circuit is uniform...fails closed' in design.md Design Decisions), satisfying criterion 3 (explicitly marked risk the human/reviewer can evaluate).

design.md has a '## Constraint Compliance' section (line 1450) which explicitly addresses the 3-runtime parity constraint (decision v2 §7) and the no-new-plugin constraint. requirements.md contains no performance/regulatory numeric-threshold constraints (grep for 'must not/shall not/within N ms/maximum N/compliant with/compatible with/SLA/latency/throughput/concurrency' in requirements.md returns no substantive hits beyond an unrelated 'must not contradict' sentence about not redefining Epic A1's schema, itself addressed in Non-goals/Data Plan/Dependencies). requirements.md's own '## Security Boundaries' section (no new trust boundary, no HMAC signing, provider-name neutrality/AC-043) and REQ-008 (version-mutation self-check) are the feature's remaining explicit constraints; both are addressed in design.md, just under separate headings rather than restated inside '## Constraint Compliance' itself: design.md's own '## Security Boundaries' (line 1413) explicitly states 'See requirements.md Security Boundaries — this design introduces no new trust boundary...', expanded fully in security-spec.md's Trust Boundaries/STRIDE tables (B1-B5); REQ-008 is addressed in '## Deployment / CI Plan' ('scripts/bump-version.sh's existing release gate is unaffected... REQ-008'). No requirements.md constraint is contradicted or silently dropped anywhere in the design; the gap is purely organizational (not consolidated under the Constraint Compliance heading), which does not create an implementation-mismatch risk given the explicit cross-references. Advisory (non-blocking): a future revision could fold the Security Boundaries/REQ-008 compliance statements into the Constraint Compliance section itself for a fully self-contained record.

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. A grep across requirements.md for numeric latency/throughput/concurrency/SLA/response-time thresholds returns no hits; this feature ships only static schema-conformance validators and a comparator CLI over small, hand-authored fixtures, with no throughput or latency target stated anywhere in requirements.md.

design.md's '## Deployment / CI Plan' (line 1429) names the deployment target (GitHub Actions test.yml + local dev, 'wired the same way Epic A2's generate-gate-capabilities.py --check is wired'), the CI pipeline changes required (six new tests/*.tests.sh/.ps1 suites registered directly in tests/run-all.sh/.ps1, plus a vendored-copy '--check' drift gate extended to 3 filenames, plus a human-copy-staged .github/workflows/test.yml registration under specs/epic-192-a4-facet-manifest/human-copy/ with a MANIFEST.sha256, per AC-033), and states 'scripts/bump-version.sh's existing release gate is unaffected' (REQ-008). infra-spec.md's CI/CD Sequence section reiterates this in full detail. Feature-flag strategy is not applicable — this feature ships static contract files and validator/comparator scripts with no incremental user-facing rollout. Migration execution order relative to deployment is not applicable because Data Plan (design.md line 247) states 'Migration Strategy: none. Every artifact this epic defines is wholly new; no existing schema, script, or content file is migrated in place' — there is no schema change to an existing table/column to sequence against a deploy.

design.md's '## Data Plan' (line 247) states 'Existing Data Affected: none... it only reads their already-fixed shapes' and 'Migration Strategy: none. Every artifact this epic defines is wholly new; no existing schema, script, or content file is migrated in place' — equivalent to the check's own 'No data changes' PASS condition. This feature adds three brand-new contract files and four brand-new scripts; it declares no column/table change to any existing artifact (contracts/capability-registry.schema.json, contracts/project-context.schema.json, contracts/approval-sidecar.schema.json, or contracts/workflow-state-registry.schema.json are explicitly named as read-only inputs this feature does not modify, requirements.md Non-goals).

design.md's '## External Integrations' (line 1423) states 'None. This feature has no dependency on any provider API, cloud service, or external network resource' and this is consistent with infra-spec.md's Deployment Topology ('No cloud, no region, no network boundary'). The one cross-script/cross-epic call this feature does make — invoking Epic A1's canonicalize-sdd-yaml as a local subprocess — is identified with name, purpose, contract, and failure behaviour: frontend-spec.md's Dependencies table names it ('Epic A1 canonicalizer (canonicalize-sdd-yaml, imported as a subprocess...)', purpose, 'Internal (same repository); no external package'), and design.md's 'YAML parse contract' (line 649) and security-spec.md's B1 boundary row both fix its failure behaviour precisely: 'A non-zero canonicalizer exit is surfaced as this validator's own diagnostic ... never silently swallowed or retried with a fallback parser', with a named diagnostic id (canonicalizer-invocation-failed) and a named test (TEST-031).

Every design.md Components entry maps to a named REQ: facet-manifest.schema.json/REQ-001, capability-summary.schema.json/REQ-002, context-projection.schema.json/REQ-003, the four validator/comparator scripts and six test suites/REQ-004/REQ-006, the storage-location convention/REQ-007, and the documentation-discipline items/REQ-008 (Deployment / CI Plan, Global Constraints). No component, data model, or endpoint appears in design.md without a REQ backing it; design.md explicitly excludes exactly what requirements.md's Non-goals exclude (no Capability Resolver, no resolve-project-context implementation, no Registry/ownership-scoped weakening detector, no full-track Capability Summary shape, no new ADR) — see Technical Summary line 9-10 and Architecture diagram's 'Epic A5, out of scope' box. Conversely, every Goal (REQ-001 through REQ-008) and every User Story (Epic A5's implementer, Epic A3's check-component-coverage, a reviewer reading a Manifest, a maintainer investigating staleness, the validator's CI/human caller) has a corresponding design.md component or contract section addressing it.

A genuine high-risk surface is present: the Policy-Weakening fail-closed staleness boundary (compare-facet-manifest-staleness's Block/fail-closed logic, an Elevation-of-Privilege/Tampering-relevant control per security-spec.md's STRIDE row for boundary B3) which, if implemented incorrectly, could let a policy weakening silently pass as fresh/stale. design.md names a concrete verification path for it: a dedicated test suite file, tests/facet-manifest-staleness.tests.{sh,ps1} (Test Strategy item 5, design.md line 1048), with specific acceptance-test IDs enumerated (AC-019, AC-020, AC-023, AC-024, AC-039, AC-040, AC-044-AC-046) and security-spec.md's Security Tests table naming the exact TEST IDs (TEST-023, TEST-024, TEST-040, TEST-044, TEST-046) and expected results for each. The discovery/vendored-copy integrity boundary (B4) and provider-neutrality boundary (B5) are likewise each given a named test (AC-032/TEST-032, AC-043/TEST-043). No high-risk surface was found to lack a named verification path.

There is no domain-layer artifact for this feature's design to conform to or diverge from, so this check's scoped risk surface is absent.
